title: Bicester Market Square Project: town-centre public realm regeneration
description: Cherwell District Council reported progress on its Bicester Market Square regeneration and public realm improvement project and approved the concept design to proceed to the next stage (design development, planning permissions and procurement), with an updated overall project budget.
statusRationale: The concept design has been approved to progress, but delivery remains subject to planning and procurement.
locationDescription: Market Square, Bicester, Oxfordshire (town-centre Market Square public realm regeneration site).
locationSource: Project description and currentLocationDescription provided (Cherwell District Council) referencing 'Market Square, Bicester, Oxfordshire'.

evidence.title: Executive agenda: Bicester Market Square Project (report and appendices)
evidence.summary: Executive meeting documents describing the Market Square regeneration/public realm improvement project, including recommendations to approve the concept design and proceed to subsequent stages subject to planning permission and procurement.

title: Ellesmere Port town centre regeneration: work starts on £13m programme
description: Cheshire West and Chester Council announced the start of construction on its Ellesmere Port town centre regeneration programme, including public realm improvements, walking/wheeling/cycling connectivity and enabling town centre investment.
statusRationale: Construction has started, but the regeneration programme remains in delivery.
locationDescription: Ellesmere Port town centre, Cheshire West and Chester (representative town centre point).
locationSource: Cheshire West and Chester Council press release: 'Work begins on Ellesmere Port town centre regeneration' (2025-03-18).

evidence.title: Work begins on Ellesmere Port town centre regeneration
evidence.summary: Council press release confirming work has started on a £13m programme to regenerate Ellesmere Port town centre, outlining the scope of improvements and intended outcomes.

title: Seaton Hole coastal defence scheme: cliff and beach protection works
description: East Devon District Council delivered a coastal defence scheme at Seaton Hole involving new rock armour and repairs to existing defences to reduce erosion and coastal cliff risk and help protect nearby homes and infrastructure.
statusRationale: The scheme is reported as completed in 2025.
locationDescription: Seaton Hole beach / West Walk Promenade area, Seaton, East Devon, Devon (site centroid).
locationSource: Project location description and title referencing 'Seaton Hole' and 'Seaton Coastal Defence Scheme' (South West Flood & Coastal project page: https://swfloodandcoastal.org.uk/project/seaton-coastal-defence-scheme/).

evidence.title: Seaton Coastal Defence Scheme 2025
evidence.summary: Project page describing the Seaton Hole coastal defence works (including rock armour installation and repairs), including start/completion dates and scheme scope and budget, with East Devon District Council listed as lead authority.

title: The Charter, Gravesend: contractor appointed to complete build-to-rent development
description: Gravesham Borough Council announced the award of a contract to Curo Construction to complete The Charter in Gravesend, a build-to-rent development with 242 apartments and a multi-storey car park in the town centre.
statusRationale: A contractor has been appointed and works are underway, but completion was stated as expected in early 2026.
locationDescription: Gravesend town centre, Gravesham Borough (representative point for The Charter build-to-rent development site in Gravesend).
locationSource: Gravesham Borough Council announcement about 'The Charter, Gravesend' town-centre development (https://www.gravesham.gov.uk/news/article/160/curo-construction-awarded-contract-to-complete-construction-of-the-charter-gravesend).

evidence.title: Curo Construction awarded contract to complete construction of The Charter, Gravesend
evidence.summary: Council news confirming contract award to complete The Charter development, describing the scheme (242 apartments and a multi-storey car park) and expected completion timeframe.

title: Rewilding Harborough: Tin House Farm land purchase and long-term restoration vision
description: Harborough District Council acquired Tin House Farm as part of its Rewilding Harborough initiative, setting out plans for habitat restoration, nature recovery and community access as the scheme progresses.
statusRationale: The council has acquired the land and published a restoration vision, with delivery to follow over multiple phases/years.
locationDescription: Harborough District, Leicestershire (representative central point for the district; Tin House Farm not precisely located from provided evidence)
locationSource: Harborough District Council news item on Rewilding Harborough and Tin House Farm acquisition (https://www.harborough.gov.uk/news/article/1878/councils_reveal_25-year_vision_to_bring_nature_back_to_district)

evidence.title: Councils reveal 25-year vision to bring nature back to district
evidence.summary: Council news describing the Rewilding Harborough programme and a long-term vision linked to the acquisition of Tin House Farm for habitat restoration and nature recovery.

title: Maidstone East: plans approved for redevelopment of former Royal Mail site
description: Maidstone Borough Council reported that planning permission has been granted for redevelopment of the former Royal Mail sorting office site near Maidstone East, supporting regeneration and new development in the area.
statusRationale: Planning permission has been approved, with delivery dependent on subsequent build-out.
locationDescription: Approximate centroid for the former Royal Mail sorting office site area near Maidstone East railway station, Maidstone, Kent.
locationSource: Maidstone Borough Council news item referencing the former Royal Mail site at Maidstone East (no precise address given); coordinates set to Maidstone East station area as the closest clear named site.

evidence.title: Plans approved for redevelopment of former Royal Mail site at Maidstone East
evidence.summary: Council news update confirming approval of redevelopment plans for the former Royal Mail site at Maidstone East.

title: Milford-on-Sea Hurst Road East seawall repairs: phase 1 completed
description: New Forest District Council completed phase 1 repairs to the Hurst Road East car park seawall at Milford-on-Sea, with further works described as planned for spring 2026.
statusRationale: Phase 1 repairs are reported complete, but additional phases are described as pending.

evidence.title: Phase one of Milford seawall repairs completed

title: Warm homes retrofit: £18.4m programme to upgrade North Kesteven council homes
description: North Kesteven District Council secured funding for a multi-year retrofit programme to improve energy efficiency and reduce carbon emissions in its housing stock through upgrades such as insulation and low-carbon heating measures.
statusRationale: Funding has been secured and delivery is planned across multiple years.
locationDescription: North Kesteven District, Lincolnshire (representative point near Sleaford, the district’s main town, for council housing stock retrofit programme)

evidence.title: Major funding boost to retrofit council homes
evidence.summary: Council announcement of £18.4m funding for a programme to retrofit and improve the energy performance of council homes over several years.

title: Basingstoke town centre and Basing View: investment partnership to accelerate regeneration
description: Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council progressed plans to procure a long-term investment partnership to accelerate improvements across Basingstoke town centre and the Basing View business district, aligned to its adopted Town Centre Masterplan.
statusRationale: The council is progressing procurement for a partner; delivery depends on securing the partnership and subsequent project implementation.
locationDescription: Basingstoke town centre / Basing View area, Basingstoke, Hampshire (representative central point near Basingstoke railway station and Basing View business district).
locationSource: Project description and council release referencing “Basingstoke town centre and the Basing View business district” (Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council).

evidence.title: Progressing plans for bold partnership to unlock major investment
evidence.summary: Council news release describing plans to seek an investment partner to fast-track improvements across Basingstoke town centre and Basing View.

title: Five Acres regeneration: funding extension for improvements to local estate and facilities
description: Forest of Dean District Council reported that the grant for the Five Acres regeneration project had been extended, enabling continued delivery of improvements and works through to March 2026.
statusRationale: Funding was extended, indicating continued delivery rather than completion.
locationDescription: Five Acres estate area, Berry Hill/Coleford, Forest of Dean District, Gloucestershire
locationSource: Project described by Forest of Dean District Council as the 'Five Acres regeneration project' in the Five Acres area (Coleford/Berry Hill). Coordinates are a representative centroid for the Five Acres estate area.

evidence.title: Five Acres regeneration project sees grant extended until March 2026
evidence.summary: Council update confirming the Five Acres regeneration project grant extension through March 2026.

statusRationale: Planning applications have been submitted, with delivery contingent on approvals and subsequent construction phases.

title: Whiston Brook flood alleviation: proposed £3m CIL contribution to start works
description: Rotherham Council proposed allocating £3m of Community Infrastructure Levy (CIL) funding to the Whiston Brook Flood Alleviation Scheme, describing the planned programme of works and intent to begin works within the following year.
statusRationale: Funding allocation was proposed to enable the scheme, with works expected to follow once approvals and delivery arrangements are in place.

locationSource: Rotherham Council news item on the Whiston Brook Flood Alleviation Scheme (https://www.rotherham.gov.uk/news/article/1991/%C2%A33m-for-whiston-brook-to-start-flood-alleviation-works) and project description indicating the Whiston Brook catchment in Rotherham.

evidence.title: £3m for Whiston Brook to start flood alleviation works

title: Loughborough Town Deal: park and town centre improvement works underway

statusRationale: Works were reported as underway, with completion dependent on construction delivery.

evidence.title: Improvements underway at award-winning park and town centre
evidence.summary: Update describing the start of improvement works in Queen’s Park and in the town centre, including upgrades to paths, drainage, electrical infrastructure and lighting.
